lesson: For in-place array reversal, swap arr[lo]/arr[hi] and step lo+=1,hi-=1 while lo<hi; the function mutates and can still return the same object.
task: reverse array in place with two pointers

lesson: For a fixed-size sliding window, seed window_sum=sum(nums[:k]) then each step add nums[i] and subtract nums[i-k] instead of re-summing the slice.
task: max sum subarray of fixed size k

lesson: For variable-size sliding window on 'sum >= target', expand hi in a for-loop but shrink lo in an inner while as long as the window still qualifies.
task: smallest subarray with sum at least target

lesson: To count subarrays summing to k, keep a running prefix sum and a Counter of prefix values seen so far (seeded with {0:1}); add counts[running-k] before updating counts[running].
task: count subarrays with sum equal to k

lesson: Dutch national flag needs three pointers lo/mid/hi: on a 2-swap with hi, decrement hi WITHOUT advancing mid, since the swapped-in value from hi is still unexamined.
task: sort array of 0s, 1s, 2s in one pass

lesson: To move zeros to the end preserving order in one pass, keep a write pointer and swap nums[write]/nums[read] only on non-zero reads instead of overwriting-then-backfilling zeros.
task: move all zeroes to end in place

lesson: For minimum window substring, track one 'missing' counter (chars still needed) instead of dict equality checks, and only shrink lo while need[s[lo]] stays negative.
task: minimum window substring containing all chars of t

lesson: For trapping rain water with two inward pointers, advance whichever side has the smaller running max, since that side's max is the true water ceiling there.
task: trapping rain water with two pointers
